spec_match.rationale: Uses if(media()) breakpoint ladder for colour, font-size, padding tokens — the canonical use case in the spec.

interactive.rationale: Resizable stage with buttons; all three tokens update together as viewport width changes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises a resizable stage updating three tokens together; page does that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show: container queries via if(container()), reduced-motion combined with prefers-color-scheme, the same ladder expressed pre-v137 (for contrast).

design_system.rationale: All variables, no raw hex in this concept.

openQuestions.title: Add an `old way` comparison
openQuestions.detail: Authors will ask `is this really shorter than @media`? Show the equivalent pre-v137 three-block @media expansion side by side.

openQuestions.title: Add a container query lane
openQuestions.detail: if(container()) is one of the killer use cases — a token that responds to its container, not the viewport. Add a resizable sub-region and rebind one of the tokens to it.

summary: Clean spec demo; adding the `old way` side-by-side would make the brevity argument explicit.
